raw_text: Enterprise client requested a demo of our full platform. Budget confirmed at 50,000 EUR annually. CFO and CTO both attending the call.
Large logistics company needs end-to-end workflow automation for 200+ staff. Urgent timeline, go-live required in 6 weeks. CTO contacted us directly via LinkedIn.
Fortune 500 retail company requires immediate ERP integration. Legal and procurement teams already engaged. Formal RFP issued. Budget 120,000 EUR.
Repeat client looking to expand current contract. Currently paying 1,800 EUR per month. Satisfied with results and wants to add three new automated workflows.
Healthcare group managing 12 clinics wants to automate patient intake and reporting. Compliance team involved. Decision maker is the COO.
Series B startup, 80 employees, raised 15M EUR last month. CTO looking to replace manual operations with AI-driven automation. Budget approved for Q2.
Global manufacturing firm needs automation across 5 departments. Vendor evaluation underway. Our solution is shortlisted. Meeting with VP Operations next week.
Private equity firm automating deal flow management. Partner-level sign-off already given. Wants to start immediately. Budget 80,000 EUR for full implementation.
Regional bank wants to automate loan application processing. Compliance and IT sign-off complete. Pilot project budget 40,000 EUR with full rollout to follow.
E-commerce company processing 10,000 orders per day needs automated exception handling and escalation routing. Technical team ready to integrate immediately.
Insurance company wants AI to triage claims before human review. Already using competitor, actively evaluating switch. Decision in 30 days. Legal team briefed.
Telecoms operator automating customer churn prediction and outreach. Data team already mapped integration points. Pilot budget confirmed at 25,000 EUR.
Law firm with 200 lawyers needs document automation and contract review pipeline. Managing partner involved. Timeline 3 months. RFP sent.
Logistics startup backed by 30M EUR funding round needs to scale operations without hiring. Automation-first mandate from board. CEO reaching out directly.
Pharmaceutical company requires automated regulatory document processing. Compliance officer and CIO both on initial call. Six-figure budget pre-approved.
Student researching AI tools for a university dissertation on automation trends in SMEs.
Someone asked a general question about pricing on the website. Left no contact info and no company details.
Small startup with 3 people exploring automation options. Budget is under 500 EUR. No specific timeline or use case defined yet.
Freelancer asking if the platform supports solo users. No company, no team, no stated budget.
Someone browsing the blog clicked the contact form and asked a general question about what AI automation means.
High school teacher curious about AI for classroom projects. Not a business inquiry.
Retired professional exploring tech trends. No business context. No budget. Just curious.
Person asking if there is a free tier. No company mentioned. No use case. Just wants to try it for free.
NGO volunteer asking about discounted pricing for non-profits. No budget confirmed, no decision maker, exploratory only.
Intern at a small company doing competitive research on automation tools. No purchasing authority. Report going to a manager.
Director at a financial services firm. Interested in the solution but said budget is frozen until Q3. Asked to follow up in 4 months.
Agency partner wants to resell the solution to their clients. No direct end-user budget. Asking about white-label and referral commission terms.
Asking detailed technical questions about architecture. No stated business need. Possibly a competitor benchmarking.
Journalist writing an article about AI automation tools. Wants a product demo for editorial purposes. No purchase intent stated.
Consultant evaluating tools on behalf of a client. Will not disclose the client name. Budget and timeline unknown until client approves.
Manager at a mid-size company mentioned interest in automation. Seemed engaged but vague on specifics. No budget or timeline confirmed.
Company with 500 employees, someone from IT reached out. Not sure if they have budget authority. Wants to explore options.
Operations director left a voicemail expressing interest. Has not responded to follow-up emails. Company looks like a good fit but engagement is cold.
Enterprise company, 1000+ staff. Someone booked a demo but gave minimal information about their use case. Could be a great fit or wrong department contact.
Head of digital transformation at a large retailer. Showed interest at conference but gave a personal email not corporate. Follow-up has been slow.
asdflkjh random gibberish text with no meaning whatsoever 123 abc xyz
Small startup exploring options. Budget under 2,000 EUR total. No specific timeline.
Mid-size company looking at options. Reasonable budget. Some urgency.
Enterprise client, confirmed budget, decision maker involved, urgent timeline.
Interested in your product
Company with genuine need and some budget signals but vague on decision maker. Could go either way.
Operations manager with decent budget, clear use case, but no urgency and no executive sponsor confirmed.
Interested company but budget approval still pending. Good fit technically, weak on commercial readiness.
CFO from a fintech company resubmitting after initial contact 2 weeks ago. Now has board approval and confirmed budget of 60,000 EUR.
CFO from a fintech company follow-up submission. Budget increased to 75,000 EUR. Wants to start next month.

We are a global enterprise operating across 47 countries with over 25,000 employees. Our operations team has been evaluating automation vendors for the past 18 months. We have a dedicated transformation budget of 2.5 million EUR for this fiscal year. The use cases include: automated procurement approval workflows, supplier onboarding automation, invoice processing, HR onboarding sequences, compliance document generation, customer escalation routing, real-time inventory alerting, predictive maintenance scheduling, contract renewal tracking, vendor performance scoring, employee expense automation, IT ticket triage, data quality validation across 12 internal systems, automated regulatory reporting for 9 jurisdictions, real-time fraud detection flagging, customer segmentation pipeline, partner commission calculation, SLA breach alerting, monthly P&L report generation, and quarterly board report compilation. Our CIO, CFO, and Chief Transformation Officer are all sponsors. We have a shortlist of 3 vendors and are conducting final evaluations this month. Decision will be made by end of quarter. We are prepared to sign a 3-year contract. Legal and procurement are already briefed and ready to move. This is our highest priority initiative for the year.
Company Name We are interested in your enterprise plan. Budget available.
Unternehmensberatung GmbH sucht eine Automatisierungsloesung fuer unser Team von 150 Mitarbeitern. Budget vorhanden. Entscheider ist der Geschaeftsfuehrer.
